req.title: System-Admin updates training-course
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a training-course through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers four distinct test dimensions: (1) a happy-path update by a valid admin (EP – valid partition); (2) submission with a blank mandatory field (NEG / DT – validation error branch); (3) a title field at its maximum allowed length boundary (BVA); (4) the state transition from 'unauthenticated' to a protected endpoint (STC / NEG – 401 path); and (5) a forbidden role attempting the update (NEG – 403 path). Together these cover positive, boundary, and multiple negative scenarios, satisfying the minimum three-test requirement while providing meaningful risk coverage. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and tear down their own course records.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_training_course_success
preconditions: An existing training-course record exists (created by fixture)
actions: Navigate to /training-courses/{course_id}/edit
Clear the Title field and enter a valid updated title
Clear the Description field and enter a valid description
Click the Save button
Capture the HTTP response and the resulting page state
expected: Response body contains the updated entity id
An audit record is written for the update action
trace: Expected outcome: training-course updated, audit record written, response 200/201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_updates_training_course_success(page, admin_session, existing_course, audit_log_reader):
    course_id = existing_course["id"]
    updated_title = "Updated Safety Fundamentals"
    updated_desc = "Refreshed content for 2024"

    page.goto(f"/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-title']", updated_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-description']", updated_desc)

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/training-courses/{course_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='save-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(course_id)

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-toast']")).to_be_visible()

    audit = audit_log_reader(entity="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record not found after successful update"

tests.name: test_admin_update_course_blank_mandatory_title_returns_400
preconditions: An existing training-course record exists (created by fixture)
actions: Navigate to /training-courses/{course_id}/edit
Clear the Title field leaving it blank (mandatory field violation)
Populate all other mandatory fields with valid values
Click the Save button
Capture the HTTP response and the resulting UI state
expected: UI displays a validation error message for the Title field
Training-course record remains unchanged in the database
trace: R-WEB-046 – error pathway: 400 on validation error
DT: decision rule where Title=blank AND other fields=valid => reject
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_update_course_blank_mandatory_title_returns_400(page, admin_session, existing_course, audit_log_reader):
    course_id = existing_course["id"]
    original_title = existing_course["title"]

    page.goto(f"/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-title']", "")
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-description']", "Some valid description")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/training-courses" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='save-btn']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-title']")).to_be_visible()

    audit = audit_log_reader(entity="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert audit is None, "Audit record must NOT be written on validation failure"

    db_title = <get_course_from_db>(course_id)["title"]
    assert db_title == original_title, "Record must remain unchanged after failed update"

tests.name: test_admin_update_course_title_at_max_length_boundary
preconditions: An existing training-course record exists (created by fixture)
Max allowed title length is 255 characters (per data dictionary)
actions: Navigate to /training-courses/{course_id}/edit
Enter a title of exactly 255 characters (max boundary)
Populate other mandatory fields with valid values
Click Save and capture the response
Repeat with a title of 256 characters (max+1 boundary) and verify rejection
expected: Title of 255 characters: HTTP 200 or 201, update accepted, audit record written
Title of 256 characters: HTTP 400, validation error shown, no audit record written
trace: R-WEB-046 – boundary case on mandatory field length
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_admin_update_course_title_at_max_length_boundary(page, admin_session, existing_course, audit_log_reader):
    course_id = existing_course["id"]
    title_at_max = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    title_over_max = "A" * (MAX_TITLE_LEN + 1)

    # --- BVA: exactly at max (should succeed) ---
    page.goto(f"/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-title']", title_at_max)
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-description']", "Valid description")
    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/training-courses/{course_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='save-btn']")
    assert resp_info.value.status in (200, 201), "255-char title must be accepted"
    audit = audit_log_reader(entity="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must exist for max-length success"

    # --- BVA: one over max (should fail) ---
    page.goto(f"/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-title']", title_over_max)
    page.fill("[data-testid='course-description']", "Valid description")
    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/training-courses" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info2:
        page.click("[data-testid='save-btn']")
    assert resp_info2.value.status == 400, "256-char title must be rejected with 400"
    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-title']")).to_be_visible()

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_training_course
preconditions: No active session cookie is present (user is unauthenticated)
An existing training-course id is known
actions: Clear all session cookies to ensure unauthenticated state
Attempt to navigate directly to /training-courses/{course_id}/edit
Observe the resulting page or API response
expected: HTTP response status is 401 or user is redirected to the login page
The edit form is not rendered
trace: STC: transition from state 'unauthenticated' to protected resource must be denied
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_training_course(browser, existing_course, audit_log_reader):
    course_id = existing_course["id"]

    # Fresh context with no cookies => unauthenticated state
    context = browser.new_context()
    page = context.new_page()

    with page.expect_response(lambda r: "/training-courses" in r.url) as resp_info:
        page.goto(f"/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")

    status = resp_info.value.status
    redirected_to_login = "login" in page.url or "sign-in" in page.url

    assert status == 401 or redirected_to_login, (
        f"Expected 401 or login redirect, got status={status} url={page.url}"
    )

    assert page.locator("[data-testid='course-title']").count() == 0, \
        "Edit form must not be visible to unauthenticated user"

    audit = audit_log_reader(entity="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record must be written for unauthenticated attempt"

    context.close()

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_updating_training_course
preconditions: User is authenticated with role 'viewer' (not admin)
An existing training-course record exists (created by fixture)
actions: Authenticate as a user with role 'viewer'
Attempt to navigate to /training-courses/{course_id}/edit
Attempt a direct PUT/PATCH API call to the endpoint with viewer credentials
Observe HTTP response status and UI state
expected: UI shows an access-denied or forbidden message
No audit record is written for the attempted update
trace: R-WEB-046 – error pathway: 403 if role forbidden
NEG: actor with insufficient role must be denied at both UI and API layers
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_non_admin_role_forbidden_from_updating_training_course(page, viewer_session, existing_course, api_client, audit_log_reader):
    course_id = existing_course["id"]
    original_title = existing_course["title"]

    # --- UI layer check ---
    page.goto(f"/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")
    forbidden_visible = (
        page.locator("[data-testid='forbidden-message']").count() > 0
        or "403" in page.title()
        or "access denied" in page.content().lower()
    )
    assert forbidden_visible, "UI must show forbidden/access-denied for viewer role"
    assert page.locator("[data-testid='save-btn']").count() == 0, \
        "Save button must not be rendered for viewer role"

    # --- API layer check (direct call with viewer token) ---
    response = api_client.patch(
        f"/training-courses/{course_id}",
        json={"title": "Hacked Title"},
        headers=viewer_session["auth_headers"]
    )
    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403 from API, got {response.status_code}"

    db_title = <get_course_from_db>(course_id)["title"]
    assert db_title == original_title, "Record must remain unchanged after forbidden attempt"

    audit = audit_log_reader(entity="training-course", entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record must be written for forbidden attempt"
